zh.lifeSummary: Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale是Sir William De Comyn Constable Of Scotland的兒子，於1115年出生在英國蘇格蘭莫雷。他於1145年與Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾巴登結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1170年被列為justiciar of lothian。他去世於1177年在英國諾圖波藍德廷達爾享年62歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭洛包爾凱爾索。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale是Sir William De Comyn Constable Of Scotland的儿子，于1115年出生在英国苏格兰莫雷。他于1145年与Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl在苏格兰音巴內-爾巴登结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1170年被列为justiciar of lothian。他去世于1177年在英国諾圖波藍德廷達爾享年62岁，埋葬在苏格兰洛包爾凱爾索。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Comyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English, Irish:  see  Cumming  .

variants.name: Cumming
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): most probably a habitational name from Bosc-Benard-Commin in Eure, France. This place lies on the edge of the Forêt de Rouvray, near Rouen, in the heart of the district from which came numerous Domesday barons, and so is much more likely to be the source of the name than Comines in Nord. The name is also possibly derived from the personal name Cumin, which is found in Norfolk, Lincolnshire, and Yorkshire in the 12th and 13th centuries and may be of Breton origin, according to Reaney. Compare Old Breton Cunmin, which also appears as Cumin, and is from con- ‘hound’ with an uncertain second element, perhaps the min ‘edge’ which is found in other personal names.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cuimín (or Ó Cuimín) ‘son (or ‘descendant’) of Cuimín’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of cam ‘crooked’. There was a 7th-century abbot of Iona named Cumin. In Scotland, this Irish surname became confused with the Norman name.

History: A notable bearer of the Norman surname is Wilhelmus Comyn, Bishop of Durham, who came north to Scotland with King David I in 1124 and was appointed Chancellor of Scotland. He established his nephew Richard in Tynedale and Roxburghshire, and Richard also rose to be Chancellor of Scotland. The Comyn or Cumming family gained the earldoms of Buchan and Menteith and rose to be the most powerful noble family in Scotland in the century before the rise of King Robert Bruce.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Tynedale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Sussex and Nottinghamshire): variant of   Tindall  . This surname is also established in Jamaica.

variants.name: Tindall
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lincolnshire): habitational name from Tynedale, the valley of the river Tyne, or from a place in Cumbria called Tindale, which is situated on a tributary of the South Tyne. The name derives from a British river name Tina (apparently from a Celtic root meaning ‘to flow’) + Old English dæl or Old Norse dalr ‘valley’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale was born in 1115, in Moray, Scotland, United Kingdom as the son of Sir William De Comyn Constable Of Scotland. He married Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl in 1145, in Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. In 1170, his occupation is listed as justiciar of lothian. He died in 1177, in Tindale, Northumberland, England, at the age of 62, and was buried in Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale est né en 1115, à Moray, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, fils de Sir William De Comyn Constable Of Scotland. Il a épousé Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl en 1145, à Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. En 1170, sa profession de son indiquée est justiciar of lothian. Il est décédé en 1177, à Tindale, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Kelso, Roxburghshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale wurde 1115 in Moray, Schottland, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Sir William De Comyn Constable Of Scotland geboren. Er hat 1145 in Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1170 ist sein Beruf als justiciar of lothian aufgeführt. Er starb 1177 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Tindale, Northumberland, England und wurde in Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedaleは1115年にイギリススコットランドモーアイでSir William De Comyn Constable Of Scotlandの息子として生まれた。1145年にスコットランドインバーネス-シュアイアバデノチでHextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Athollと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1170年，職業はjusticiar of lothianと記録されている。彼は1177年にイギリスノースウンバーランドティンダルで62歳で死去し，スコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアケルソにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale(은)는 1115년 영국 스코틀랜드 모라이에서 Sir William De Comyn Constable Of Scotland의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1145년 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르 바데노치에서 Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1170년, 그의 직업은 justiciar of lothian이었다. 그는 1177년 영국 노르스벌랜드 필데일에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 록스버그h실르 켈소에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale nasceu em 1115, em Moray, Escócia, United Kingdom filho de Sir William De Comyn Constable Of Scotland. Ele casou-se com Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl em 1145, em Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Em 1170, sua profissão aparece como justiciar of lothian. Ele faleceu em 1177, em Tindale, Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard De Comyn Lord Of Tynedale родился в 1115 г. в местности мореи, Шотландия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Sir William De Comyn Constable Of Scotland. Он женился на женщине по имени Hextilda fitz Uchtred Countess Of Atholl в 1145 г. в местности баденох, инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. В 1170 году он работал по профессии justiciar of lothian. Он умер в 1177 г. в местности тиндеил, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности келсо, роксбургшир, Шотландия.

En 1170, su ocupación registrada era justiciar of lothian. Murió en 1177, en Tindale, Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland.
